title: Andryala aurea
intro: Andryala aurea, also known as Golden Andryala, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Andryala aurea include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Andryala aurea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Andryala aurea is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the many flowering plants that are characterized by their composite inflorescence. The genus Andryala itself is part of the tribe Cichorieae, which includes other plants like the chicory and the endive. Within the Asteraceae family, Andryala aurea is further grouped into the subfamily Cichorioideae. This classification indicates its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family and its shared characteristics.
